cards.hook: "Women aren't even shallow" — Nietzsche's cruelest line
cards.caption: "Women are considered profound. Why? Because we never fathom their depths. But women aren't even shallow." Nietzsche at his most savage. The line my feed pulled before the doomscroll buried it.
cards.stance: Half of Nietzsche's edgelord bangers are just him losing an argument with himself.

cards.hook: He defended a master's thesis on whether Calvin & Hobbes is philosophy
cards.caption: Someone defended a Tilburg master's thesis arguing a comic book can BE philosophy — and won. Asking the question is answering it. The kind of gem my feed keeps and the timeline never surfaces.
cards.stance: Calvin & Hobbes did more real epistemology than most intro-to-philosophy syllabi.

cards.hook: Marcus Aurelius on why you keep misreading people
cards.caption: "Direct thy attention to what is said. Let thy understanding enter into the things that are doing." Marcus Aurelius on actually paying attention. Stoicism the feed kept before it scrolled past.

triggers: inside-baseball academic drama, low clarity, no standalone value

cards.hook: "What are you gonna do with that?" Ask a philosophy major
cards.caption: The comeback for smug "what will you do with a philosophy degree?" people: "dress up marketing for AI firms as profound philosophy ahead of their IPOs." Brutal. The take my feed saved from the scroll.
cards.stance: Every AI lab's mission statement is a philosophy BA's revenge on the people who mocked them.

cards.hook: Studying philosophy turned out to be absurdly useful
cards.caption: "Studying philosophy turned out to be consistently, ridiculously useful for me." Not the pitch you were sold about the useless degree. The quiet contrarian take my feed kept for you.
cards.stance: Philosophy is the most practical degree nobody will admit is practical.

triggers: no context, references a speech not shown, zero standalone clarity

cards.hook: Hannah Arendt: you don't actually want truth, you want to stop not-knowing
cards.caption: "To expect truth to come from thinking signifies that we mistake the need to think with the urge to know." Arendt on why your brain conflates the two. The line my feed kept before it vanished.
cards.stance: Most of what people call 'seeking truth' is just discomfort with open questions.

cards.hook: Philosophy runs on far-fetched scenarios — with a straight face.
cards.caption: Philosophy runs on far-fetched thought experiments, told with a straight face. The absurd hypotheticals are the method, not a bug. The stuff my feed keeps before the timeline buries it.
cards.stance: The weirdest thought experiments do the most real philosophical work.

cards.hook: A veganism debate got heated on YouTube.
Hire the problem solver — you can't teach that later.
cards.caption: This game dev hires for one thing: thoughtful problem solvers. Technical skills you can teach anyone. Analytical thinking you can't. 162 likes agree. The takes my feed keeps before the timeline buries them.
cards.stance: Skills are trainable; judgment isn't — hire for the thing you can't install.

cards.hook: There's a phenomenology of lying — banal vs non-banal.
cards.caption: A free philosophy journal breaks down 'narrative breakdown' — a full phenomenology of banal and non-banal lying. The niche gems my feed surfaces before the timeline ever shows them.
cards.stance: Not all lies are equal — some are structurally worse than others.

cards.hook: The philosophy behind 'all men are created equal.'
Socialist philosophy, yes please.
"We are strong so our burdens stay light." — Stoicism.
cards.caption: Seneca: strength exists so your burdens feel light. The whole Stoic move in one line — you build capacity for the weight you'll carry. The quiet gems my feed keeps before the timeline scrolls past.
cards.stance: Stoicism isn't about avoiding hardship — it's about outgrowing it.

cards.hook: The city you walk shapes how you think.
cards.caption: Psychogeography: the psychological impact of urban space on your mind. Merlin Coverley's book, reviewed. Where philosophy meets the streets you actually live in. The stuff my feed keeps before the timeline buries it.
cards.stance: Your environment edits your thoughts more than you notice.

cards.hook: Want to write well? Epictetus gave the whole method.
cards.caption: Want to write well? Epictetus: "If you would be a good reader, read; if a writer, write." No hack. Just the loop. Stoic gold my feed keeps.
cards.stance: Every writing course is a footnote to one Stoic line.

cards.hook: Philosophy is important. But it's okay.
god bless cow vtubers for explaining philosophy
You quote the isms — but have you ever practiced them?
cards.caption: You quote the isms. But have you practiced them in the purest form? "Reading is fundamental. When did we stop pushing that idea?" The take my feed kept.
cards.stance: Naming a philosophy isn't the same as living it.

cards.hook: Marcus Aurelius: stop imagining, stop pulling your own strings.
cards.caption: Marcus Aurelius: "Wipe out the imagination. Stop pulling the strings. Confine thyself to the present." 2,000 years old, still the cure for spiraling. Stoic gold my feed keeps.
cards.stance: Half your anxiety is imagination pretending to be planning.

cards.hook: The Declaration of Independence is applied philosophy.
cards.caption: The Founders were steeped in one question: defining Natural Law. That's why the DOI matters — inalienable rights and consent of the governed are Enlightenment principles. Found before the timeline.
cards.stance: The DOI is a philosophy paper that happened to found a country.

cards.hook: Plato: obsessing over health blocks the practice of virtue.
cards.caption: Plato: "Attention to health is a great obstacle to the practice of virtue." A shot at wellness culture 2,400 years early. The take my feed kept.
cards.stance: Optimizing your body can become a way to avoid improving your character.

cards.hook: Raymond Moody on NDEs, science and philosophy
Writing like John Locke now reads as anti-intellectual
cards.caption: Writing like John Locke now reads as "anti-intellectual." Careful thinking got recast as elitism, and that should bother you. A Locke passage my feed surfaced before the timeline buried it.
cards.stance: We started calling depth pretentious and mistook that for progress.

cards.hook: "The music that can deepest reach is cordial speech."
cards.caption: "The music that can deepest reach, and cure all ill, is cordial speech." Emerson, on why kind words outlast every argument you'll win online. One line my feed kept out of the noise.

cards.hook: Reason or revelation, pick one — just don't fake reconciling them
cards.caption: Reason or revelation: pick your ground and stand on it. What you can't do is fake reconciling both and call it rigor. A sharp take on faith vs reason my feed surfaced first.
cards.stance: Most "faith and reason in harmony" arguments are just refusing to pick.

cards.hook: Analytic philosophy: the one field with no norm against name-calling
cards.caption: Analytic philosophy is famously the one field with no norm against just calling colleagues wrong by name. Fodor on why that's a feature, not a bug. The kind of hot take my feed keeps.
cards.stance: A discipline that tolerates being told you're plainly wrong stays honest longer.

cards.hook: The philosophy syllabus that starts with animal and AI minds
cards.caption: A philosophy of mind course that opens with animal minds, artificial minds, and how the brain actually computes. This is where consciousness meets AI. The kind of thread my feed surfaces first.
cards.stance: Philosophy of mind and AI research are the same question wearing two badges.

cards.hook: All of ancient Chinese political thought in one messy chart
cards.caption: Daoism, Legalism, and the rest of ancient Chinese political thought mapped in one gloriously messy hand-drawn chart. Confusion cleared. A gem my feed pulled out of 5,000 posts.
